Traffic Arbitrage:
Buying Email Ads
About Buying Email Ads:
System Description: Email 	advertising 	can 	produce 	large
amounts of traffic to your website. This system will focus on how
to research these email opportunities, how to find media kit and
rate card information, as well as basic e-mail guidelines. After
completion of this system you should have an understanding on
where you can find e-mail advertising lists.
Required Skills: Necessary skills required include basic comput-
er and internet skills, ability to follow instructions, can research
websites using Google and other search engines, can contact and
negotiate with ad reps and website owners.
System Checklist:
Step 1: Traditional Email List Renting, Video 1
Action Steps:
1. Search Google for your industry/niche email list.
2. Research categories within different “email renting
companies.”
3. Locate pricing information and contact ad rep.
4. Ask for open rates, negotiate pricing (NEVER PAY
RATE CARD).
5. Place orders and never be happy with the results.
6. Ask for a “make good” to make up for any poorly
Weekly Action Plan:
Day 1 Task Checklist:
Research e-mail companies.
Research websites with news-
letters.
Request media kit and rate
card information.
Day 2 Task Checklist:
Review rate card and media
kit information.
Contact website owners.
Contact ad reps and discuss
rates and deadlines.
Day 3 Task Checklist:
Write the email ad copy in-
house.
Day 4 Task Checklist:

-- 1 of 2 --

performing mailings.
ImPoRTANT: Take note of any AdSense Ads and
target those sites using Placement Targeting.
Step 2: Alternative Email Advertising Opportunities,
Video 2
Action Steps:
1. Search Google for your industry/niche.
2. Locate websites about the topic.
3. Look for newsletter opt-in boxes.
4. Locate contact information.
5. Contact website owner and strike a deal.
ImPoRTANT: Take note of any AdSense Ads and tar-
get those sites using Placement Targeting.
Step 3: Email Guidelines and Suggestions, Video 3
Action Steps:
1. Keep the message short.
2. Strike curiosity of the reader – leave them hanging.
3. Call to action – sell the click (provide multiple links
to squeeze or sales page).
System Checklist:
Tips for Making the Most of This Traffic System:
When looking for e-mail opportunities don’t limit your mar-
keting research to your specific industry or niche. Research and
seek out complimentary products and services to yours and ad-
vertise there as well.
www.Rent-A-List.com
www.Arcamax.com
www.Emarketingoptin.com
www.InfoUSA.com
Weekly Action Plan:
Research email companies.
Research websites with
newsletters.
Request media kit and rate
card information.
Day 5 Task Checklist:
Review rate card and media
kit information.
Contact website owners.
Contact ad reps to discuss
rates and deadlines.
